data.title: Infosys Raises Full-Year Revenue Forecast as Large Deals Pick Up
data.description:
Infosys lifted its full-year revenue growth guidance, a move that could suggest the long downturn in global IT spending is beginning to soften as clients gradually return to technology investment. The company now expects constant-currency revenue growth of 3% to 3.5% for the fiscal year ending March 2026, up from its prior 2% to 3% outlook and slightly above the average market expectation. Chief executive Salil Parekh said the improved view reflects a pickup in large deal activity over recent quarters and stronger execution, particularly across financial services, energy, utilities, and resource sectors, where Infosys has increasingly positioned itself as an AI partner.

The revised outlook follows an extended period of muted demand for Indian software services firms after the post-pandemic surge faded, especially in the US and Europe. Elevated interest rates, inflation, and geopolitical tensions have constrained corporate technology budgets, pushing companies like Infosys to lean more heavily into artificial intelligence and cloud services to support growth. For the December quarter, revenue rose 8.9% to 454.79 billion rupees, while net income declined 2.3% to 66.54 billion rupees, partly due to the impact of India's new labor codes. The company also added 5,043 employees during the quarter, a sequential increase that could point to improving demand visibility.

Looking ahead, Infosys said it continues to serve US clients using a blend of local hiring and offshore delivery as Indian IT firms prepare for possible disruption from changes to the H-1B visa regime under US President Donald Trump. Parekh said about 90% of the company's largest 200 clients are now engaged in AI-driven work, and he expects the technology to contribute meaningfully to growth over the coming years. He added that Infosys could consider acquisitions as larger AI services specialists begin to emerge, potentially strengthening its position as global demand for AI-led IT services continues to develop.

data.title: These Stocks Are Today's Movers: Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Citigroup, Tesla, Rivian, Intel, AppLovin, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni and George Glover

Stocks were falling Wednesday as investors processed the latest quarterly earnings from major U.S. banks and the absence of a ruling from the Supreme Court on President Donald Trump's tariffs.

These stocks were making moves:

Bank of America fell 4.9%. Fourth-quarter earnings of 98 cents a share topped the 96 cents that analysts expected. Revenue of $28.4 billion also beat the $27.76 billion analysts had anticipated, according to FactSet.

Wells Fargo fell 5.5%. The bank posted fourth-quarter earnings of $1.62 a share, missing Wall Street estimates of $1.66. Quarterly revenue rose 4% to $21.3 billion, though the number came in below forecasts. Results included a $612 million severance charge, Wells Fargo said.

Citigroup declined 3.1%. Excluding the sale of Citi's Russian operations, revenue grew 8% in the fourth quarter. The bank warned investors last month that the deal would result in an after-tax loss of $1.1 billion.

Netflix fell 1.5%. The video streamer is weighing an all-cash offer for Warner Bros. Discovery's streaming and studios assets, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter. Netflix is trying to get the deal done quickly as rival Paramount Skydance carries on pushing its competing bid. Netflix declined to comment on the possible revised offer. Warner Bros. Discovery fell slightly.

Tesla declined 1.9%. CEO Elon Musk said Wednesday that the electric-vehicle maker would stop selling advanced driver-assistance features for a one-time payment in favor of a monthly subscription service.

Rivian Automotive slid 8.9% to $17.17. UBS downgraded shares of the EV maker to Sell from Neutral, even as it raised its price target to $15 from $13. Analysts said the market's appetite for Rivian's AI efforts may have reached its peak following a 15% rally for the stock since Dec. 11.

Nvidia slipped 1.8%. The chip maker secured U.S. approval to ship its H200 artificial-intelligence chips to China, but it remains to be seen whether Beijing will allow sales to go ahead. Chinese companies would be willing to buy around 1.5 million of the H200 chips, representing roughly $30 billion in revenue, according to KeyBanc analyst John Vinh.

Honeywell International rose 0.6%. Quantinuum, the quantum computing company majority-owned by Honeywell, said Wednesday that it plans to submit a draft registration statement for a proposed initial public offering.

Fortinet and Palo Alto Networks fell 1.5% and 0.4%, respectively. Chinese authorities have told local companies to stop using cybersecurity software made by a dozen U.S. and Israeli companies, Reuters reported, citing two people briefed on the matter. Fortinet and Palo Alto Networks didn't respond to requests for comment from Barron's.

Intel added 3.3% and was set to extend its gains from Tuesday's session, when it closed up 7.3%. The chip maker caught an upgrade from KeyBanc's Vinh on Monday, who cited strong demand from cloud service providers.

Intuitive Surgical fell 5% following a fourth-quarter earnings pre-announcement. The maker of surgical robots said worldwide procedures completed using its da Vinci and Ion systems increased roughly 18% from the fourth quarter of 2024.

U.S.-listed shares of Trip.com Group sank 17% after the so-called Expedia of China said it faced an antitrust probe by the country's top business regulator.

Glaukos, the maker of ophthamalic medical technology, tumbled 8.6%. The company posted $45 million in net iDose sales in its fourth quarter, below the $48 million to $50 million Wall Street had expected.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com and George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tesla's Full Self-Driving System Will Only Be Available Via Subscription, Musk Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Mauro Orru and Becky Peterson

Tesla Chief Executive Elon Musk said the electric-vehicle maker would stop selling a suite of advanced driver-assistance features for a one-time payment, switching instead to a monthly subscription service.

The company's Full Self-Driving system makes it easier for drivers to change lanes and navigate around other vehicles and objects, but doesn't make vehicles fully autonomous and is meant to be used only with a fully attentive driver.

Tesla currently offers the FSD system for a one-time payment of $8,000 or a subscription of $99 a month in the U.S., according to Tesla's website.

Musk wrote in a post on X that Tesla would stop selling the system after Feb. 14, making it only available through a monthly subscription. He didn't elaborate on the decision to remove the one-time payment option. Tesla shares were recently trading down 2.5%.

The company is shifting its business toward autonomous vehicles and robotics and away from primarily selling electric vehicles, which currently make up three-fourths of its revenue. Tesla sales fell 9% in 2025 and 16% for the fourth quarter compared with a year prior. The company lost its crown as the world's leading EV maker to the Chinese company BYD.

Tesla is looking to grow its recurring subscription revenue, which opens its business up to the high-margin sales seen across the software industry. As of late 2025, around 12% of Teslas on the road had monthly or lifetime subscriptions to FSD, the company said.

For years, Musk has talked up the potential for FSD to transform every Tesla on the road into a fully-autonomous vehicle. He has said that in the future Tesla owners will be able to add their personal vehicles into Tesla's robotaxi fleet, where the vehicles will autonomously transport people around town, generating income for the car's owner.

While Tesla has made progress toward autonomy, the commercially available version of FSD isn't there yet. The company is currently testing autonomous vehicles on a limited basis in Austin, Texas, where it offers ride-hailing services in the city with a safety driver in the passenger seat.

The version of FSD available to customers can navigate streets and autopark, but it can disengage if something goes wrong, requiring the driver to quickly take over.

Some Tesla customers paid as much as $15,000 in a one-time fee for lifetime access to FSD, with the expectation that their cars would eventually get software upgrades that made it fully autonomous.

But Tesla owners with older cars aren't able to use the most recent releases of the software because they have older hardware on their cars.

Tesla has acknowledged that it will need to make things right for those customers by offering hardware upgrades but hasn't committed to when or how that will happen.

"We do want to solve autonomy first and then we'll come back with a way to take care of these customers," Chief Financial Officer Vaibhav Taneja said on an October investor call. "These customers are very important. They were the early adopters."

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com and Becky Peterson at becky.peterson@wsj.com

data.title: ServiceNow Treasury Contract Shrunk on Federal Employee Exits — Market Talk
data.description: Treasury Department reduced spending on a contract with ServiceNow by around $15 million as a result of federal employee resignations, Stifel analysts write in a note. The reduction stems from the Deferred Resignation Program implemented by DOGE that gave federal employees paid leave if they agreed to resign on Sept. 30, 2025. The analysts say this appears to be one of the first major contract reductions as a result of the program, but predict that the ServiceNow contract is likely not the only one affected, noting that the impact from the September resignations are only now registering in federal contracting data. "We believe there is the potential that more of these DRP-related contractions are likely to surface as an incremental CY26 growth headwind for many enterprise software seat-based models in coming months/quarters," they write. ServiceNow falls 3.1%, extending Tuesday's losses. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: BofA clients tune out of equities overall, but tune in to communication services
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes fall; Nasdaq off most, down ~1.5%
  • Tech weakest S&P sector; Energy leads gainers
  • Dollar dips; gold gains; crude up >1%; bitcoin rallies ~3%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield dips to ~4.14%

BOFA CLIENTS TUNE OUT OF EQUITIES OVERALL, BUT TUNE IN TO COMMUNICATION SERVICES

BofA Securities equity and quant strategist Jill Carey Hall says that last week, with the S&P 500 index advancing about 1.6%, clients sold U.S. equities for a sixth week in a row, selling both single stocks (-$2.2 billion; sixth-straight week of outflows) and equity ETFs (-$0.4 billion; first outflows since early October of last year).

"Selling was led by institutional clients (4th wk. of selling) while hedge fund clients bought for the 4th straight wk. Retail clients bought US equities after selling for the past 9 weeks," writes Hall in her note.

She adds that after record inflows in 2025, small caps were the only size segment to see inflows in the first week of 2026.

Additionally, corporate client buybacks accelerated to their highest weekly level since April, but trailing 52-week buybacks as a percentage of market cap are at their lowest since early 2024 and in line with the average since 2010.

Hall says that clients sold stocks in seven out of eleven sectors, led by Industrials, which saw their largest outflows since December 2018, and Financials (which has seen outflows in eight out of the past nine weeks).

Tech also had outflows for a second week in a row.

Communication Services captured the biggest inflows, its largest ever as a GICS sector since 2018. Staples, Energy and Materials also garnered inflows.

In terms of ETFs, Hall indicated that clients sold equity ETFs across major styles (Growth/Value/Blend). Large- and mid-cap ETFs saw outflows, while small-cap/broad market ETFs had inflows.

Clients bought ETFs in 10 of 11 sectors last week, led by Energy.

Tech was the sole sector to see outflows.

data.title: Wells Fargo CFO Urges Careful Consideration of a Trump Credit-Card Interest Rate Cap — Market Talk
data.description: Wells Fargo CFO Mike Santomassimo sidesteps a question about President Trump's call to temporarily cap credit-card interest rates, stating instead that the bank is willing to work with governing bodies to address affordability problems currently plaguing some consumers. "What the right response to that is something that we do think should be carefully considered," he says on a call with analysts. Santomassimo adds that it's too early to tell how things will play out. "But we're very much aligned with trying to find solutions to help as many as we can, and just do it in a way that doesn't have adverse impact," he says. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: CTMX: Vaseta M and CX801 advance in late-line CRC and melanoma, with major data updates expected in 2026
data.description:
The conference highlighted progress on two lead oncology programs: Vaseta M, an EpCAM-targeting ADC showing promising efficacy and safety in late-line colorectal cancer, and CX801, a masked interferon alpha-2b advancing in melanoma. Key milestones include a 100-patient Vaseta M update by Q1 and initial CX801 combination data by end of 2026.

Based on

data.title: German general 'shaken' by abuse cases in elite paratroop unit
data.description:
Germany's most senior general said on Wednesday he had been deeply shaken by a scandal over alleged sexual abuse, right-wing extremism and drug use at an elite paratroop unit and promised to strengthen leadership in the army.

"We do not tolerate sexualised violence, extremism, drug abuse or discrimination in the armed forces," the commander of Germany's military, General Carsten Breuer told reporters in Berlin, saying the incidents have "shaken me deeply".

"Soldiers who condone such behaviour cannot serve as officers in our armed forces," he said, after presenting a report on the affair to the parliamentary defence committee.

The scandal emerged after female soldiers in the 26th Parachute Regiment, whose missions have included helping the evacuation of Germans and other foreign nationals from Sudan, reported numerous cases of alleged abuse to the Parliamentary Commissioner for the Armed Forces, the military ombudsman.

The affair has piled pressure on the military as Germany begins registration of all 18-year-olds as part of a drive to increase the size of the armed forces - a response to U.S. demands for European NATO allies to shoulder more of the burden of defending their continent.

The opposition Green party accused Defence Minister Boris Pistorius of seeking to avoid responsibility for the alleged abuse, which the party's deputy parliamentary leader Agnieszka Brugger said had taken root under his conservative predecessors.

"This is not only a disastrous signal, but a real danger to increasing the personnel of the Bundeswehr and thus to the security of us all," she said in a statement.

The military says a series of disciplinary measures has already been taken, including dismissal of a number of soldiers, and 16 cases had been handed over to civil prosecutors.

"We have certainly gained the impression that the investigations were in some cases not conducted with the necessary depth," General Christian Freuding, the senior army general, said.

data.title: Rice Holds Near 2-Month Highs
data.description:
Rice futures traded above $10.5 per hundredweight, holding near two month highs as tighter near term availability and renewed spot demand extended the rebound from early January lows.

In the US, downward revisions to production and imports alongside stronger domestic and residual use have sharply reduced projected ending stocks, constraining readily available long grain supplies that underpin nearby pricing.

At the same time, importers in South Asia and Africa have returned to the market to secure prompt shipments as trade frictions and payment disruptions linked to geopolitical tensions, including interruptions to basmati flows involving Iran, increased urgency in the front months.

These near term pressures have outweighed the looser global backdrop of higher stocks and output in China and Japan and expanded Indian exports, which continue to cap upside but have not alleviated short dated supply strain.

data.title: US DOJ closes investigation of Swedbank
data.description:
Swedbank said on Wednesday that the U.S. Department of Justice has closed its investigation into the bank without enforcement.

The investigation was initiated in 2019 and related to Swedbank's historical anti-money laundering work, the company said.

data.title: Lapin Kumi, part of Citira, has acquired Daniek
data.description:
Lapin Kumi, Citira’s Finnish tire services chain, has today acquired Daniek, an established provider of tire services in Tammisaari, southern Finland. The acqusition continues to grow the Lapin Kumi service network across major transport routes in Finland.

“This acquisition strengthens Lapin Kumi’s position in southern Finland and adds a new service location in Tammisaari to our network. At the same time, we are expanding into a Swedish-speaking region and will be able to serve an even broader customer base, including customers coming from the Helsinki metropolitan area. Tammisaari forms an important southern link for us,” says Ville Ruokanen, CEO of Lapin Kumi.

Daniek is a company with a long history of providing tire-related services, including tire swaps and sale of tires and rims, founded in 1990 with roots dating back to 1986 in Tammisaari.

“We have extensive experience and a long history in the industry, which provides a strong basis for the future. Through Lapin Kumi, we will gain even better resources to develop our operations. Business will continue in Tammisaari as usual, and good customer service will remain our top priority,” says Mats Danielsson of Daniek.

Daniek will continue to operate in its current location with the same team, as part of Lapin Kumi. The current owners will become co-owners of Citira.

Contact

Ville Roukanen, CEO Lapin Kumi & Head of Finland Citira

Email: press@citira.com

About Lapin Kumi

Lapin Kumi is an independent tire service chain in Finland, founded in Roveniemi and run by the Ruokanen family since 1947. The current CEO, Ville Ruokanen, represents the third generation in the family business. Ville’s grandfather, Veikko Keskiruokanen, started the company by repairing rubber boots. Lapin Kumi is part of Citira.

About Citira

Citira is a circular tire management provider. Our company offers tire service, collection and retreading of worn-out tires, as well as sale and delivery of replacement tires. Our inhouse logistics enables a circular flow of products – delivering replacement tires, while collecting used casings.

Citira strives to make tire management convenient and sustainable for transport companies and vehicle users. Our company supports customers in gaining more uptime, reduce environmental impact, lower costs, and meet safety requirements.

Citira consists of 120+ directly operated service stations, 39 affiliated service stations, 5 retreading sites, 1000+ employees and inhouse logistics covering Northern Europe. Our mission is to keep society moving in a convenient, sustainable way.

For more info visit citira.com and follow us on Linkedin.

Attachments

Lapin Kumi, part of Citira, has acquired Daniek

data.title: Trustpoint Xposure Named The Only AEO-Certified Agency Delivering PR Visibility in AI Search Results
data.description:
Trustpoint Xposure has been recognized as the only AEO-certified agency purpose-built to convert public relations into measurable visibility within AI search results, responding to a fundamental change in how brands are discovered online.

POST FALLS, IDAHO / ACCESS Newswire / January 14, 2026 / As AI assistants increasingly generate answers instead of lists of links, brand visibility now depends on whether machines can interpret, trust, and reference information. Trustpoint Xposure was formed to address this shift by redesigning PR workflows around AEO, authority signals, and consistency across the web.

A Structural Shift in Public Relations

Rather than treating PR as a channel for exposure alone, Trustpoint Xposure approaches it as a system for machine validation. The agency's process ensures that media coverage, brand messaging, and factual signals are aligned so AI systems can confidently surface brands in response to user questions.

This evolution reframes media placement from a visibility tactic into a credibility input, one that helps AI determine which brands are reliable enough to cite.

Why AEO Certification Matters

AEO certification reflects an agency's ability to structure information in ways that answer engines can process. Trustpoint Xposure applies this discipline across PR campaigns, ensuring that brand mentions, definitions, and claims remain consistent and verifiable across sources.

Reputation Signals in an AI Context

Trustpoint Xposure also integrates reputation management into its AI-first PR model. Inconsistent narratives and conflicting information reduce trust for both humans and machines. By aligning messaging across earned media, owned content, and third-party references, the agency strengthens long-term brand credibility in AI evaluations.

This consistency supports a durable presence as AI systems reassess sources continuously.

Education for the AI Search Transition

To help leaders understand this changing landscape, Trustpoint Xposure has released its E-book, How Do I Rank First in AI Search, which outlines the principles behind AI-readable authority, structured PR, and sustainable visibility in answer-driven search environments.

The book serves as a practical guide for brands adapting to a future where being cited matters more than being clicked.

About Trustpoint Xposure

Trustpoint Xposure is an AEO-certified agency that helps brands achieve visibility in AI search results by aligning public relations with Answer Engine Optimization, authoritative media placement, and reputation-focused strategy. The agency specializes in building brand signals that AI systems can understand, trust, and reference.

Jack Smith

Media Manager

Trustpoint Xposure

contact@trustpointxposure.com

SOURCE: Trustpoint Xposure

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Taylor Bank Welcomes Tyler McCalister as Commercial Loan Officer
data.description:
BERLIN, MD / ACCESS Newswire / January 14, 2026 / Taylor Bank announced today that Tyler McCalister has recently joined the organization as Commercial Loan Officer and Vice President, supporting the Bank's expansion of commercial lending services in Rehoboth Beach, Lewes, and the broader Sussex County, Delaware market.

"Tyler's extensive experience and deep understanding of commercial lending will be invaluable as we strengthen our presence in Sussex County," said Douglass M. Cook, Chief Lending Officer, Executive Vice President at Taylor Bank. "We are excited to expand into the Greater Rehoboth and Lewes markets to better serve local businesses and communities."

McCalister brings more than 12 years of extensive banking experience to Taylor Bank, including expertise in commercial lending, business development, credit risk, and private wealth management. His career includes roles at prominent financial institutions such as JPMorgan and CIT/One West Bank in New York, as well as Banc of California in Los Angeles.

A graduate of the University of Delaware with a Bachelor of Science in Finance, McCalister recently returned to his home state and is eager to contribute to the growth and success of Delaware's business community. He is currently based at Taylor Bank's corporate office in Berlin, Maryland as he integrates with the team and prepares to serve clients throughout Sussex County.

Taylor Bank invites business owners and community stakeholders to connect with Mr. McCalister and learn more about Taylor Bank's commercial lending capabilities designed to support local businesses. He can be reached at (302) 381-6591 or email at tmccalister@taylorbank.com.

About Calvin B. Taylor Banking Company

Calvin B. Taylor Banking Company, the bank subsidiary of Calvin B. Taylor Bankshares, Inc. (OTCQX:TYCB), founded in 1890, offers a wide range of loan, deposit, and ancillary banking services through both physical and digital delivery channels. The Company has twelve full-service banking locations and one loan production office within the eastern coastal area of the Delmarva Peninsula including Worcester County and Wicomico County, Maryland, Accomack County and Northampton County, Virginia and Sussex County, Delaware.

Contact

Tori Grundman, Marketing Director, AVP

410‑641‑1700, taylorbank.com

SOURCE: Calvin B. Taylor Bankshares, Inc.

data.title: Polymarket Lists War-Related Contracts as Regulators and Lawmakers Push Back
data.description:
Growing regulatory scrutiny is starting to converge on Polymarket as it expands further into contracts tied to active military conflicts. In recent weeks, the platform has listed markets linked to scenarios such as a potential Chinese invasion of Taiwan, Russian advances in Ukraine, and possible US strikes on Iran, with one Iran-related contract generating more than $18 million in trading. The move stands out in a sector where many peers have historically avoided direct wagers on war, and it comes as Polymarket deepens its connection to traditional finance following a commitment by Intercontinental Exchange Inc. to invest up to $2 billion in the company.

Political and industry pressure has intensified alongside that growth. A group of 12 Democratic senators, led by Catherine Cortez Masto, has argued that federal law expressly prohibits contracts tied to war, warning that such markets could be manipulated or potentially exploited by foreign adversaries. The American Gaming Association has raised parallel concerns, noting that similar contracts would not be permitted under state or tribal law. By contrast, rivals such as Kalshi Inc. have said they avoid war-related markets altogether due to the harmful incentives they could create, while ForecastEx, owned by Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR), has said CFTC rules prohibit contracts linked to terrorism, assassination, or war.

The regulatory backdrop remains uncertain as prediction markets scale. Polymarket has technically been off-limits to US customers since a 2022 settlement with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, operating instead through crypto-based accounts that allow pseudonymous trading. The company is now preparing to launch a US platform after acquiring a CFTC-approved exchange, with its beta emphasizing sports-related contracts. While military and geopolitical markets account for less than 10% of Polymarket's total volume, according to user-compiled data from Dune Analytics, they have contributed to recent growth as global conflicts intensify, highlighting how prediction markets could continue to test regulatory boundaries as they become more integrated with the financial system.

data.title: Denmark to Ramp Up Military Presence in Greenland as U.S. Talks Conclude — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton

Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with with Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen and Greenlandic Foreign Minister Vivian Motzfeldt at the White House to discuss the fate of Greenland.

The meeting lasted for about an hour. Shortly beforehand, both sides doubled down on their divergent positions regarding the future of the Danish territory.

The Danish Ministry of Defence said Wednesday that there would be an expanded military presence in and around Greenland "in close cooperation with NATO allies." It added that the purpose was to train and strengthen NATO's footprint in the Arctic region.

Denmark has said that the U.S. has " no legal basis" to annex Greenland, though President Donald Trump has argued that the U.S. needs the territory for national security reasons. The Arctic island's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen said Tuesday, "If we have to choose between the USA and Denmark here and now, we choose Denmark."

Greenland's Prime Minister added that "the purpose is to train the ability to operate under the unique Arctic conditions and to strengthen the alliance's footprint in the Arctic, benefiting both European and transatlantic security."

This morning Trump reiterated his view that the U.S. must acquire Greenland. "It is vital for the Golden Dome that we are building," he posted on Truth Social early Wednesday.

The Golden Dome is a space-based missile defense system that the U.S. is looking to create. Strategically, Greenland is well-situated for national security purposes because the shortest route for a ballistic missile from Russia to the U.S. is over Greenland

The U.S. military base in Greenland, the Pituffik Space Base, was renamed in 2023 in part to reflect the traditional Greenlandic name for the area where the base is located. The U.S. Space Force is responsible for protecting satellites, missile warning systems and other space based operations.

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: North Sea Crude - Gunvor bids for several grades
data.description:
North Sea and U.S. WTI Midland crude differentials rose again as Gunvor continued with persistent buying interest, joined by Azerbaijan's Socar.

PLATTS WINDOW

  • Indications are free on board (FOB) unless marked as cost, insurance and freight (CIF) or delivered at place.

  • There were no deals on Wednesday.

  • WTI Midland: Gunvor bid for three cargoes on a one cancels the other (OCO) basis, the strongest of which was for January 26-February 5 dates at dated Brent plus $3.20 CIF Rotterdam. That equated to around dated plus $1.98 on a FOB basis, Reuters calculations showed.

  • On Tuesday, Gunvor had bought February 13-17 dates from Equinor at dated plus $2.90 CIF Rotterdam.

  • Forties: Gunvor bid for January 24-February 7 loading at dated plus $1.95.

  • That was up from a bid in the previous session, when Gunvor bid for January 23-February 7 at dated Brent plus $1.65.

  • Forties: Socar bid for February 8-14 at dated plus $1.85.

  • Troll: Gunvor bid for a cargo loading January 24-31 at dated plus $3.10, up from Tuesday's bid when the trading house sought January 23-February 15 at dated plus $2.80.

  • Oseberg: Gunvor bid for January 24-February 2 dates at dated plus $3.00, up from its Tuesday bid, for January 23-February 15 at dated Brent plus $2.65.

  • Ekofisk: Gunvor bid for two cargoes, one with January 24-27 dates and the other loading February 2-11, at dated plus $2.70.

  • On Tuesday, Gunvor bought a January 28-30 cargo from Glencore at dated Brent plus $2.40.

  • Brent: Socar bid for a cargo loading February 11-13 at dated plus $1.85. On Tuesday, Gunvor bought February 11-13 from BP at dated Brent plus $1.60.

  • Gunvor's Troll, Oseberg and Ekofisk bids were all on an OCO basis. Socar's bids for Forties and Brent were also OCO.
